title: rubus serpens subsp. oreades
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. oreades, also known as the mountain dewberry, is a subspecies of the dewberry plant that is native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus serpens subsp. oreades include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing antioxidant protection against cell damage. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
